race.name: Kananaskis Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Rocky Mountain wilderness skiing through Alberta's Kananaskis Country — big peaks, cold air, Canadian grit.

vitals.aid_stations: Multiple stations with water, electrolyte drink, and snacks

climate.description: Late January in the Canadian Rockies brings reliably cold conditions. Temperatures typically -25°C to -10°C. Chinook winds can occasionally bring sudden warming, but deep cold is the norm at this elevation.
challenges: Extreme cold possible — wind chill below -35°C in exposed sections
Chinook winds can cause rapid temperature swings
Short daylight hours in late January at this latitude

course.primary: Loops and out-and-back sections through Kananaskis Country's groomed trail network in the Rocky Mountain foothills. Rolling terrain through lodgepole pine and spruce forest with views of the Rockies. The course follows the provincial park trail system with moderate elevation changes.
course.grooming: Provincial park grooming, well-maintained but basic compared to World Cup standards
features: Stunning Rocky Mountain backdrop throughout the course
Trails wind through old-growth spruce and lodgepole pine forest
Wildlife sightings possible — elk, deer, and occasionally moose
Post-race gathering at the Kananaskis visitor centre
Part of a broader winter recreation area with extensive trail networks

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A scenic Rocky Mountain marathon with reliable snow and dramatic mountain views. Small field and limited international profile keep it in the regional tier, but the setting is world-class.

history.summary: A relatively young race established in the mid-2000s to showcase the Kananaskis Country trail system for competitive Nordic skiing. The event draws primarily from Alberta's active Nordic community, particularly Calgary-area clubs. Kananaskis has hosted provincial-level competitions and the marathon builds on the region's history as a training venue for Canadian national team members.
notable_facts: Kananaskis Country hosted events during the 1988 Calgary Winter Olympics
The trail system is one of the most extensive groomed networks in the Canadian Rockies
Draws primarily from Calgary and Alberta Nordic clubs
Elevation gives the course a true mountain skiing character

videos.title: XC Ski | Ribbon Creek, Kananaskis
videos.description:
Cross-Country Ski Ribbon Creek, Kovach and Terrace Loop located in Kananaskis.
The loop is 5.6 km
Elevation gain of 226 m 

Location | Ribbon Creek, Kovach and Terrace Loop, Kananaskis.

Appreciate your support! 
📺👉SUBSCRIBE - https://www.youtube.com/c/MakingTracks?sub_confirmation=1

Canada Adventure Vlogs. https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLJ5jXspOBlsr9tN8cPKoEemnTqCOXFrqy

Follow us on Instagram!
http://www.instagram.com/isabelvalverderuiz 
http://www.instagram.com/thejourneyofmike

Making Tracks is a travel couple who have been adventuring around the world on and off since they first met in the summer of 2017. 

#canadatourism #kananaskis #crosscountryskiing

videos.title: Cross Country ski race Catamount Half marathon 28k in Steamboat Springs 2020!
videos.description:
Cross Country ski race Catamount Half marathon 28k in Steamboat Springs in March 2020!  Filmed with my Sony I made a race day vlog to film and show some of the fun races that we have here in Colorado and Utah. I hope you enjoy and have a great day!

This is a very edited down video of the race.  Thanks for watching!

Thanks for everyone who helped put this race together and I highly recommend it for anyone next year!

Let me know if you have any questions about the race, skis, gear, conditions, etc. in the comments below. Also any feed back on the video would be great!

Sorry for the wind noise, its a first edition GoPro so I tried to hide it with loud songs.

Contact for business inquiries: eriklynchskis@gmail.com

Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/eriklynchskis/

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/eriklynch_ski

Thanks! 

I hope you get some great skiing!!

videos.title: Finlandia Cross-Country Ski Marathon Set To Begin
videos.transcript:
if you're in the mood for skiing this
weekend the Finlandia ski marathon is
the thing to do the CrossCountry ski
Marathon welcomes both beginners and
expert skiers and consists of five races
skiers from all over the country are
expected to participate including Canada
for the spectator sport it can take
anywhere from 1 to three hours to
complete the race and participants go
around the course two to three times
this year three there will be some
changes to the
course we decided to Short in the course
this year um it's normally a 25k Loop um
and we've gone down to an 11 11k across
the road here or what we call the west
side of buav Vista Ski Area now due to
the warm weather the start times for the
race have been moved up by 1 hour and
will now begin at 9:00 a.m. if you've
enjoyed this segment of Lakeland news
please consider making a tax deductible
contribution to Lakeland Public
Television

but the weather did make for a perfect

videos.title: Built for Speed: Calgary January 2014 xc ski race
videos.description: Tough and fun day of racing for Sasha on January 11, 2014 (Bib 223): the weather changed twice that day: we started with a mild -5C; with the warm front moving in in time for the first race the temperature moved up to +5C only to plummet again for the last race down to a snowy and windy -7C!!! So, you can see him dressed in three different outfits: from barely anything to the warmest down parka we have!
